untranslatable_reason: BARR Bottom pattern requires manual identification of three parallel trend lines and their specific geometric relationships. This is a structural/visual pattern that cannot be expressed using point-in-time technical indicators. The pattern relies on: (1) constructing three parallel trend lines at specific intervals, (2) identifying the lowest trend line position, and (3) detecting when price breaks below it. These multi-bar trend line constructions and their spatial relationships are not available in the allowed indicator set. A full implementation would require custom trend line drawing logic or pattern recognition not supported by standard OHLC indicators.
translation_notes: Attempted simplified approximation using pivot points and price action, but this does not capture the true BARR Bottom pattern as described. The pattern is fundamentally a manual charting technique requiring trend line analysis that exceeds the scope of indicator-based rules.
